original.title: 65 Doctors, Nurses and Paramedics: What We Saw in Gaza
original.summary: New York Times interactive feature presents testimonies from doctors who worked in Gaza during the conflict.

signals.page_title: Opinion | What Doctors and Health Care Workers in Gaza Saw - The New York Times
signals.text_snippet: SKIP TO CONTENT SKIP TO SITE INDEX Section Navigation Search LOG IN OPINION GUEST ESSAY 65 Doctors, Nurses and Paramedics: What We Saw in Gaza Daniel Benneworth-Gray By Feroze Sidhwa Dr. Sidhwa is a trauma and general surgeon who worked at the European Hospital in Khan Younis, Gaza, for two weeks in March and April. Oct. 9, 2024 Share full article 440 I worked as a trauma surgeon in Gaza from March 25 to April 8. I’ve volunteered in Ukraine and Haiti, and I grew up in Flint, Mich. I’ve seen violence and worked in conflict zones. But of the many things that stood out about working in a hospital in Gaza, one got to me: Nearly every day I was there, I saw a new young child who had been shot in the head or the chest, virtually all of whom went on to die. Thirteen in total. At the time, I assumed this had to be the work of a particularly sadistic soldier located nearby. But after returning ho

signals.text_snippet: Skip to main content The homepage Explainers Politics Culture Advice Listen Watch SIGN IN BECOME A MEMBER MENU Is Israel committing genocide? Reexamining the question, a year later. The context you need, when you need it When news breaks, you need to understand what actually matters — and what to do about it. At Vox, our mission to help you make sense of the world has never been more vital. But we can’t do it on our own. We rely on readers like you to fund our journalism. Will you support our work and become a Vox Member today? Join now POLITICS Is Israel committing genocide? Reexamining the question, a year later. We spoke to a number of experts a year ago. original.title: Amos Goldberg: 'What is happening in Gaza is a genocide because Gaza does not exist anymore'
original.summary: Le Monde publishes opinion piece by Amos Goldberg arguing that the situation in Gaza constitutes genocide.

signals.page_title: Amos Goldberg: 'What is happening in Gaza is a genocide because Gaza does not exist anymore'
signals.text_snippet: Date: Saturday, May 9, 2026 Time: 11:08 am (Paris) Go Back to the HomePage Le Monde Go Back to the HomePage Le Monde FR EN Subscribe Menu Search Search War in Gaza Trump's plan explained Five scenarios for the future States recognize Palestine The genocide question OPINION OPINION OPINION WAR IN GAZA WAR IN GAZA WAR IN GAZA Amos Goldberg: 'What is happening in Gaza is a genocide because Gaza does not exist anymore' Given the scale of the massacres and destruction perpetrated by the Israeli army in Gaza, the Israeli historian, a specialist of the Holocaust, says in an interview with Le Monde that his country 'criminally overreacted' to the October 7 massacres. signals.page_title: 100,000 Dead: What We Know About Gaza's True Death Toll - Gaza News
signals.text_snippet: Navigation Login SUBSCRIBE Haaretz - back to home page Search Accessibility Current section Gaza News 100,000 Dead: What We Know About Gaza's True Death Toll Gift this article Share to Facebook Share to X Print article Comments: 124 Save Zen Reading A list of Gazans killed in the war. The first 937 names are of babies less than a year old. The death toll in Gaza, as reported by the Palestinian Health Ministry, understates the true scale of the crisis, researchers say. Hunger, disease, and Israeli gunfire at food distribution centers have made the war in the Strip one of the bloodiest of the 21st century Nir Hasson Follow June 26, 2025 On Monday of this week, Hamas' Health Ministry in the Gaza Strip published an updated list of those killed in the war, a 1,227-page chart, arranged from youngest to oldest. original.title: Enough is Enough: Israel is Committing War Crimes
original.summary: Haaretz opinion piece argues that Israel's actions in Gaza constitute war crimes and calls for accountability.

signals.page_title: Enough Is Enough. Israel Is Committing War Crimes - Opinion
signals.text_snippet: Navigation Login SUBSCRIBE Haaretz - back to home page Search Accessibility Current section Opinion Opinion Enough Is Enough. Israel Is Committing War Crimes Gift this article Share to Facebook Share to X Print article Comments: 60 Save Zen Reading Recent operations in the Gaza Strip have nothing to do with legitimate war goals. This is now a private political war Ehud Olmert Follow May 27, 2025 The government of Israel is currently waging a war without purpose, without goals or clear planning and with no chances of success. Never since its establishment has the State of Israel waged such a war. The criminal gang headed by Benjamin Netanyahu has set a precedent without equal in Israel's history in this area, too. original.title: Gaza doctor whose nine children were killed in Israeli strike dies from injuries
original.summary: BBC reports on the ongoing humanitarian crisis and conditions in Gaza during the conflict.

signals.page_title: Hamdi al-Najjar: Gaza doctor whose nine children were killed in Israeli strike dies from injuries
signals.text_snippet: Skip to content Watch Live Subscribe Sign In Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Documentaries Gaza doctor whose nine children were killed in Israeli strike dies from injuries 2 June 2025 Share Save Add as preferred on Google Tom Bennett BBC News, Jerusalem Getty Images Dr Hamdi al-Najjar - pictured during a visit by his niece - died from his wounds on Saturday, health officials said A Palestinian doctor whose children were killed in an Israeli air strike in Gaza on 23 May has died from injuries sustained in the same attack, health officials say. Dr Hamdi al-Najjar, 40, had just returned from dropping his wife, Dr Alaa al-Najjar, off at Nasser Hospital, where the couple both worked, when their home in Khan Younis was struck.
